project_title: Discovery Engine - eight-stage cross-disciplinary research-question discovery apparatus
runtime_target: local-first web or desktop application with a graph-backed research-stage dependency model
operational_end_state: A researcher can start with a seed question, discover transferable mechanisms from other fields, pressure-test analogies, move the result through academic research stages, and see downstream sections marked stale when upstream claims, methods, or evidence change.
core_modules: Observatory intake and problem decomposition | Scanner and source-field discovery | Bridge mechanism-transfer mapper | Cross-Pollination and XPol fit-pressure tester | Incubator research-object builder | Forge writing/export gate | stage-dependency stale-state engine
source_strategy: Use sources to strengthen cross-field mechanism transfer and academic-stage dependency. Sources should not merely answer a query; they should help move a mechanism from one field into another while marking downstream stages stale when upstream assumptions change.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output | dependency test: upstream change marks downstream work stale
current_build_status: file-backed proof build; needs live reviewer capture for stronger public demonstration
proof_to_full_build_gap: Convert file-backed proof into live reviewer capture and repeatable demo.
first_full_build: Implement a live seed-question walkthrough with one cross-field transfer and one downstream stale-state propagation.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
